title: How to Leash Train a Puppy (2026) – First Steps Guide
seoTitle: How to Leash Train a Puppy – First Steps | GetPawsy
seoDescription: Step-by-step puppy leash training from first collar introduction to confident outdoor walks. Age-appropriate exercises for 8–16 weeks.
h1Override: How to Leash Train a Puppy – First Steps Guide
excerpt: Age-appropriate leash training for puppies from 8 weeks. First collar introduction, indoor leash walking, and transitioning to outdoor confidence.
quickAnswer: Start leash training at 8 weeks: (1) Let puppy wear collar/harness indoors for short periods, (2) Attach a light leash and let them drag it supervised, (3) Pick up the leash and follow YOUR PUPPY (don't pull), (4) Gradually guide direction with treats. First outdoor walks: keep to 5 minutes at 10 weeks.

faq.question: When can a puppy go for walks outside?
faq.answer: After their second round of vaccinations (typically 10–12 weeks), they can walk in low-risk areas. Avoid dog parks and high-traffic areas until fully vaccinated at 16 weeks. Carry them to socialization spots before then.

sections.heading: Week 1: Introduction (8–9 Weeks)
sections.content:
**Day 1–2:** Put a lightweight collar or puppy harness on for 10 minutes while feeding or playing. Remove if puppy panics. Gradually increase wear time.

**Day 3–4:** Attach a light leash (not retractable) and let puppy drag it around the house while supervised. Treat randomly while wearing it.

**Day 5–7:** Pick up the leash. Follow your puppy. Don't pull or guide yet — just prevent tangles. Treat when puppy walks near you voluntarily.

For puppy-sized harnesses and lightweight leashes, browse our [puppy training essentials →](/collections/puppy-training-essentials).

sections.heading: Week 2–3: Building Direction (10–12 Weeks)
sections.content:
Now you start gently guiding:

1. Lure puppy beside you with a treat in your hand
2. Take 3–5 steps together → treat
3. If puppy plants or pulls → stop. Wait. Lure back to your side
4. Practice indoors first, then in your backyard
5. First outdoor walks: 5 minutes maximum, low-distraction areas

**Critical rule:** Never drag a puppy. If they plant and refuse to move, crouch down, encourage, and lure. Dragging creates leash fear.

For the complete training progression into adult leash skills, read our [Leash Training Step-by-Step Guide →](/guides/leash-training-dog-step-by-step). For complete puppy gear, browse [puppy essentials →](/collections/puppy-training-essentials).
